notes.title: Units and Measurements вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Measurement is the foundation of physics. Every physical quantity has a **magnitude** and a **unit**.

## Key Concepts
- **Fundamental (base) quantities:** length (m), mass (kg), time (s), electric current (A), temperature (K), amount of substance (mol), luminous intensity (cd).
- **Derived units:** formed from base units (e.g. speed = m/s, force = kgВ·m/sВІ = N).
- **Dimensional formula:** expresses a quantity in terms of [M], [L], [T]. Example: Force = [MВ№LВ№TвЃ»ВІ].
- **Dimensional analysis** is used to: check correctness of equations, derive relations, and convert units.
- **Significant figures:** reflect the precision of a measurement. Rules govern rounding in calculations.
- **Errors:** absolute error, relative error, and percentage error. Errors add up in sums/differences; relative errors add in products/quotients.

## Key Formulas
- Percentage error = (О”a / a) Г— 100
- For Z = (A^p)(B^q), relative error = p(О”A/A) + q(О”B/B)

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Use dimensional analysis to instantly reject wrong options in MCQs вЂ” if dimensions don't match, the formula is wrong.

notes.title: Motion in a Straight Line вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Study of motion along a straight line (1-D kinematics) without worrying about its cause.

## Key Concepts
- **Distance vs Displacement:** distance is scalar (total path), displacement is vector (shortest, with direction).
- **Speed vs Velocity:** speed is scalar, velocity is vector. Average velocity = displacement/time.
- **Acceleration:** rate of change of velocity. Uniform acceleration в†’ constant a.
- **Graphs:** slope of x-t graph = velocity; slope of v-t graph = acceleration; area under v-t graph = displacement.

## Key Formulas (uniform acceleration)
- `v = u + at`
- `s = ut + ВЅatВІ`
- `vВІ = uВІ + 2as`
- `sв‚™ = u + a(n в€’ ВЅ)` (distance in nth second)

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
For a body thrown up, take g = в€’9.8 m/sВІ and remember velocity = 0 at the highest point.

notes.title: Motion in a Plane вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Motion in two dimensions using vectors вЂ” includes projectile and circular motion.

## Key Concepts
- **Vectors:** quantities with magnitude and direction; add by triangle/parallelogram law.
- **Resolution:** any vector splits into components: `Aв‚“ = A cosОё`, `A_y = A sinОё`.
- **Projectile motion:** horizontal and vertical motions are independent. Horizontal: constant velocity; Vertical: uniform acceleration g.
- **Uniform circular motion:** speed constant but velocity changes в†’ centripetal acceleration directed to centre.

## Key Formulas
- Time of flight: `T = 2u sinОё / g`
- Max height: `H = uВІsinВІОё / 2g`
- Range: `R = uВІsin2Оё / g` (max at Оё = 45В°)
- Centripetal acceleration: `a = vВІ/r = П‰ВІr`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Range is the same for complementary angles (e.g. 30В° and 60В°).

notes.title: Laws of Motion вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Newton's three laws connect force and motion вЂ” the heart of mechanics.

## Key Concepts
- **First law (inertia):** a body stays at rest or in uniform motion unless acted on by a net external force.
- **Second law:** `F = ma`; force = rate of change of momentum `F = dp/dt`.
- **Third law:** every action has an equal and opposite reaction.
- **Momentum:** `p = mv`; conserved when net external force is zero.
- **Friction:** static (`f в‰¤ Ојв‚›N`) and kinetic (`f = Ојв‚–N`). Static friction is self-adjusting.
- **Banking of roads** and **circular motion** dynamics.

## Key Formulas
- `F = ma`, Impulse `J = FО”t = О”p`
- Conservation of momentum: `mв‚Ѓuв‚Ѓ + mв‚‚uв‚‚ = mв‚Ѓvв‚Ѓ + mв‚‚vв‚‚`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Always draw a free-body diagram (FBD) first вЂ” it makes force problems straightforward.

notes.title: Work, Energy and Power вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Relates force, displacement, and energy вЂ” including conservation principles.

## Key Concepts
- **Work:** `W = FВ·s = Fs cosОё`. Positive, negative, or zero depending on angle.
- **Kinetic energy:** `KE = ВЅmvВІ`. **Work-energy theorem:** net work = change in KE.
- **Potential energy:** gravitational `PE = mgh`; spring `PE = ВЅkxВІ`.
- **Conservation of mechanical energy:** total energy constant when only conservative forces act.
- **Power:** rate of doing work, `P = W/t = FВ·v`.
- **Collisions:** elastic (KE conserved) vs inelastic (KE not conserved, momentum always conserved).

## Key Formulas
- `W = Fs cosОё`, `KE = ВЅmvВІ`, `P = Fv`
- Coefficient of restitution: `e = (vв‚‚в€’vв‚Ѓ)/(uв‚Ѓв€’uв‚‚)`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
In a perfectly elastic collision of equal masses, the velocities are simply exchanged.

notes.chapter: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
notes.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Extends mechanics from point particles to rigid bodies that rotate.

## Key Concepts
- **Centre of mass (COM):** point where total mass can be assumed concentrated.
- **Torque:** `П„ = r Г— F`; rotational analogue of force.
- **Moment of inertia (I):** resistance to rotational change; depends on mass distribution.
- **Angular momentum:** `L = IП‰`; conserved when net external torque is zero.
- **Rolling motion:** combination of translation and rotation.

## Key Formulas
- `П„ = IО±`, `L = IП‰`
- Rotational KE = `ВЅIП‰ВІ`
- Parallel axis theorem: `I = I_cm + MdВІ`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
For conservation of angular momentum (e.g. a spinning skater pulling arms in), `Iв‚ЃП‰в‚Ѓ = Iв‚‚П‰в‚‚`.

notes.title: Gravitation вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Newton's universal law of gravitation and planetary/satellite motion.

## Key Concepts
- **Universal law:** `F = Gmв‚Ѓmв‚‚/rВІ`.
- **Acceleration due to gravity g** varies with altitude, depth, and latitude.
- **Gravitational potential energy:** `U = в€’Gmв‚Ѓmв‚‚/r`.
- **Escape velocity** and **orbital velocity** of satellites.
- **Kepler's laws** of planetary motion (orbit, areal velocity, period).

## Key Formulas
- `g = GM/RВІ`
- Escape velocity: `vв‚‘ = в€љ(2GM/R) = в€љ(2gR)`
- Orbital velocity: `vв‚Ђ = в€љ(GM/r)`
- Kepler's third law: `TВІ в€ќ rВі`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Escape velocity = в€љ2 Г— orbital velocity (at the surface). Remember vв‚‘ в‰€ 11.2 km/s for Earth.

notes.title: Mechanical Properties of Solids вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
How solids deform under applied forces вЂ” elasticity.

## Key Concepts
- **Stress:** internal restoring force per unit area. **Strain:** fractional deformation.
- **Hooke's law:** within the elastic limit, stress в€ќ strain.
- **Moduli of elasticity:** Young's modulus (Y, length), Bulk modulus (K, volume), Shear/Rigidity modulus (О·, shape).
- **Poisson's ratio:** lateral strain / longitudinal strain.
- **Elastic potential energy** stored in a stretched wire.

## Key Formulas
- `Y = stress/strain = (FВ·L)/(AВ·О”L)`
- Elastic PE per unit volume = `ВЅ Г— stress Г— strain`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Steel has a higher Young's modulus than rubber, so steel is more elastic (resists deformation more).

notes.title: Mechanical Properties of Fluids вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Behaviour of liquids and gases at rest and in motion.

## Key Concepts
- **Pressure:** `P = F/A`; increases with depth: `P = Pв‚Ђ + ПЃgh`.
- **Pascal's law:** pressure applied to an enclosed fluid is transmitted equally (hydraulic lift).
- **Buoyancy / Archimedes' principle:** upthrust = weight of displaced fluid.
- **Viscosity:** internal friction in fluids; Stokes' law for spheres.
- **Surface tension:** energy per unit area; capillary rise.
- **Bernoulli's theorem:** energy conservation in flowing fluids.

## Key Formulas
- `P = Pв‚Ђ + ПЃgh`
- Bernoulli: `P + ВЅПЃvВІ + ПЃgh = constant`
- Terminal velocity: `v = 2rВІ(ПЃв€’Пѓ)g / 9О·`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Bernoulli explains lift on aeroplane wings: faster air on top в†’ lower pressure в†’ upward lift.

notes.title: Thermal Properties of Matter вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Heat, temperature, expansion, and heat transfer.

## Key Concepts
- **Temperature scales:** Celsius, Fahrenheit, Kelvin.
- **Thermal expansion:** linear (О±), area (ОІ = 2О±), volume (Оі = 3О±).
- **Specific heat capacity** and **latent heat** (fusion, vaporisation).
- **Calorimetry:** heat lost = heat gained.
- **Heat transfer:** conduction, convection, radiation.
- **Newton's law of cooling** and **Stefan-Boltzmann law**.

## Key Formulas
- `Q = mcО”T`, `Q = mL`
- `О”L = LО±О”T`
- Stefan's law: `E = ПѓTвЃґ`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
For linear, area, and volume expansion remember the ratio О± : ОІ : Оі = 1 : 2 : 3.

notes.title: Thermodynamics вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Study of heat, work, and energy transfer in systems.

## Key Concepts
- **Zeroth law:** defines temperature (thermal equilibrium).
- **First law:** `О”Q = О”U + О”W` (energy conservation).
- **Thermodynamic processes:** isothermal, adiabatic, isobaric, isochoric.
- **Second law:** heat cannot flow spontaneously from cold to hot; introduces entropy.
- **Heat engines, refrigerators, and Carnot cycle** (max efficiency).

## Key Formulas
- First law: `О”Q = О”U + О”W`
- Work in isothermal: `W = nRT ln(Vв‚‚/Vв‚Ѓ)`
- Carnot efficiency: `О· = 1 в€’ T_c/T_h`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
In an adiabatic process О”Q = 0, so all work is done at the expense of internal energy.

notes.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Explains gas behaviour using the motion of molecules.

## Key Concepts
- **Ideal gas equation:** `PV = nRT`.
- **Pressure of a gas** from molecular motion: `P = в…“ПЃвџЁvВІвџ©`.
- **Kinetic interpretation of temperature:** average KE в€ќ absolute temperature.
- **Degrees of freedom** and **law of equipartition of energy**.
- **Mean free path** of molecules.

## Key Formulas
- `PV = nRT`
- Average KE per molecule = `(3/2)k_BT`
- RMS speed: `v_rms = в€љ(3RT/M)`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
v_rms в€ќ в€љT and в€ќ 1/в€љM вЂ” heavier molecules move slower at the same temperature.

notes.title: Oscillations (SHM) вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Periodic to-and-fro motion, especially Simple Harmonic Motion (SHM).

## Key Concepts
- **SHM:** restoring force в€ќ displacement and directed toward mean position (`F = в€’kx`).
- **Displacement:** `x = A sin(П‰t + П†)`.
- **Energy in SHM:** continuously interchanges between KE and PE; total energy constant.
- **Simple pendulum** and **spring-mass system**.
- **Damped, forced, and resonance** oscillations.

## Key Formulas
- `T = 2ПЂв€љ(m/k)` (spring), `T = 2ПЂв€љ(L/g)` (pendulum)
- Total energy = `ВЅkAВІ = ВЅmП‰ВІAВІ`
- `v = П‰в€љ(AВІ в€’ xВІ)`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Velocity is maximum at the mean position; acceleration is maximum at the extreme positions.

notes.title: Waves вЂ” Key Concepts
notes.description:
## Overview
Propagation of disturbances that transfer energy without transferring matter.

## Key Concepts
- **Transverse vs longitudinal** waves.
- **Wave equation:** `y = A sin(П‰t в€’ kx)`; relation `v = fО»`.
- **Speed of waves** on a string and sound in media.
- **Superposition:** interference, beats, and standing waves.
- **Resonance** in strings and air columns; **Doppler effect**.

## Key Formulas
- `v = fО»`, `v = в€љ(T/Ој)` (string)
- Beat frequency = `|fв‚Ѓ в€’ fв‚‚|`
- Doppler: `f' = f(v В± vв‚Ђ)/(v в€“ v_s)`

## рџЋЇ Exam Tip
Beats are heard only when the frequency difference is small (в‰¤ ~10 Hz) вЂ” useful for tuning instruments.
